questions.text: What is the minimum voting age for a citizen of India?
Which independent body is responsible for conducting free and fair elections in India?
What does EVM stand for in the context of Indian elections?
Who appoints the Chief Election Commissioner of India?
What is the maximum number of members in the Lok Sabha as per the Constitution?

myths.statement: If you don't vote in one election, your name is automatically removed from the voter list.
myths.explanation: Your name remains on the electoral roll until you move, pass away, or are disqualified. However, you should periodically check your status.

myths.statement: EVMs (Electronic Voting Machines) are not connected to any network like the internet.
myths.explanation: EVMs are standalone machines. They have no wireless communication components and are entirely disconnected from the internet, preventing remote hacking.

myths.statement: You need your Voter ID card (EPIC) to cast your vote. Without it, you cannot vote.
myths.explanation: While EPIC is preferred, you can use other approved photo identity documents like Aadhaar, Passport, or PAN card, provided your name is on the electoral roll.

myths.statement: NOTA (None of the Above) votes do not affect the election outcome.
myths.explanation: NOTA simply registers a rejection of all candidates. Even if NOTA gets the highest votes, the candidate with the next highest votes is declared the winner.

myths.statement: The Model Code of Conduct comes into effect the moment elections are announced.
myths.explanation: The MCC comes into force immediately after the Election Commission announces the election schedule, ensuring a level playing field.
